name: The Internet Login
notes: Action error overruled by success signals: Could not resolve action candidate: sign in button

findings.title: Console errors
findings.description: The page has a console error: 'Failed to load resource: net::ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED'. This could indicate a problem with a third-party resource.
findings.fix_prompt: Check the Optimizely script tag and ensure the URL is correct. If not needed, remove the script tag.

findings.title: Failed network requests
findings.description: There is a failed network request to Optimizely. This could be a tracking issue or a problem with a third-party service.
findings.fix_prompt: Verify if the Optimizely service is required. If not, remove the related script tag and tracking code.

findings.title: Missing alt text for image
findings.description: The 'Fork me on GitHub' image is missing alt text.
findings.fix_prompt: Add alt text to the 'Fork me on GitHub' image, e.g., 'Fork me on GitHub'.

raw_status.summary: The login page appears to be functional but has some minor issues related to console errors and accessibility. Overall, the page seems to be in good condition.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/login and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Console errors
   Fix: Check the Optimizely script tag and ensure the URL is correct. If not needed, remove the script tag.

2. [LOW] Failed network requests
   Fix: Verify if the Optimizely service is required. If not, remove the related script tag and tracking code.

3. [LOW] Missing alt text for image
   Fix: Add alt text to the 'Fork me on GitHub' image, e.g., 'Fork me on GitHub'.

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

name: Practice Test Automation Login
notes: Action error overruled by success signals: Could not resolve action candidate: sign in button

findings.title: Outdated copyright year
findings.description: The copyright year in the footer is outdated (2020). It should be updated to the current year.
findings.fix_prompt: Update the copyright year in the footer to the current year using HTML and update the JavaScript code that generates the year.

findings.title: Missing input field labels
findings.description: The username and password input fields are missing labels, which can affect accessibility for screen readers.
findings.fix_prompt: Add HTML labels for the username and password input fields.

findings.title: Test case inconsistencies
findings.description: Test case 1: Positive Login test has an incorrect verification step for the new page URL.
findings.fix_prompt: Update test case 1 to verify the correct URL after a successful login.

raw_status.summary: The login page is mostly well-structured and functional but has a few minor issues related to content and accessibility. Overall, the page appears to be professionally done.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://practicetestautomation.com/practice-test-login/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Outdated copyright year
   Fix: Update the copyright year in the footer to the current year using HTML and update the JavaScript code that generates the year.

2. [LOW] Missing input field labels
   Fix: Add HTML labels for the username and password input fields.

3. [LOW] Test case inconsistencies
   Fix: Update test case 1 to verify the correct URL after a successful login.

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

before_snapshot.title: Test Login | Practice Test Automation
before_snapshot.text_snippet:
Press "Enter" to skip to content
HOME PRACTICE COURSES BLOG CONTACT
Test login
This is a simple Login page. Students can use this page to practice writing simple positive and negative LogIn tests. Login functionality is something that most of the test automation engineers need to automate.
Use next credentials to execute Login:
Username: student
Password: Password123
Username 
Password 
Submit
Your username is invalid!
Test case 1: Positive LogIn test
Open page
Type username student into Username field
Type password Password123 into Password field
Push Submit button
Verify new page URL contains practicetestautomation.com/logged-in-successfully/
Verify new page contains expected text ('Congratulations' or 'successfully logged in')
Verify button Log out is displayed on the new page
Test case

after_snapshot.title: Logged In Successfully | Practice Test Automation

HOME PRACTICE COURSES BLOG CONTACT
Logged In Successfully

Congratulations student. You successfully logged in!

Log out

findings.title: Username and password are prefilled
findings.description: The username and password fields are prefilled with 'Admin' and 'admin123' respectively, which could be a security concern.
findings.fix_prompt: Remove prefilled values from username and password fields

findings.title: Missing alt text for images
findings.description: The images on the page are missing alt text, which could affect accessibility for visually impaired users.
findings.fix_prompt: Add alt text to all images on the page

raw_status.summary: The login page appears to be functional but has some minor issues. The username and password fields are prefilled, which could be a security concern, and the images on the page are missing alt text.
raw_status.bundled_fix_prompt:
I ran a reliability audit on https://opensource-demo.orangehrmlive.com/ and found the following issues.
Please fix all of them:

1. [LOW] Username and password are prefilled
   Fix: Remove prefilled values from username and password fields

2. [LOW] Missing alt text for images
   Fix: Add alt text to all images on the page

After fixing all issues, make sure the site works correctly on both desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) viewports.

findings.title: Missing form field labels
findings.description: The form fields for username and password are missing explicit labels, which can make it difficult for screen readers to interpret the form.
findings.fix_prompt: Add label elements for the username and password fields. For example: <label for='username'>Username</label> and <label for='password'>Password</label>

findings.title: Social media links lack text
findings.description: The social media links at the bottom of the form lack visible text, which can make it difficult for users to understand the purpose of the links.
findings.fix_prompt: Add visible text or ARIA labels to the social media links. For example: Follow us on Twitter or Twitter

raw_status.summary: The login form appears to be functional and visually consistent across desktop and mobile views. However, there are some minor accessibility and content issues that should be addressed to improve the overall user experience.
